title: Recall a memory-only error-handling convention in a later edit
task: In src/account.js, make `withdraw` reject overdrafts: if `amount` is greater than `account.balance`, raise an error instead of returning a negative balance.

steps.content: never `throw new Error(...)` directly — always raise failures with the `fail(code, message)` helper from src/errors.js so every error carries a machine-readable code (an eslint rule forbids bare throws of Error)

steps.task: Resume the same session and review the overdraft change against the project error convention. Fix anything still inconsistent, then report the result.

checks.fact: [convention] never `throw new Error(...)` directly — always raise failures with the `fail(code, message)` helper from src/errors.js so every error carries a machine-readable code (an eslint rule forbids bare throws of Error)

notes: Exercises a pending-to-approved memory lifecycle, exposes the approved convention in a real agent run, then resumes the same session. The exposure assertion ensures resume replay does not double-count passive memory exposure.
